zh.lifeSummary: 當ALAN DE WOODTHORPE於1180年出生在英國林可爾史爾阿福德時，他的父親William de Woodthorpe I39歲，母親Cecilia de Craon46歲。他於1180年與Beatrice Farforth在英國英格蘭林可爾史爾吉爾頓 在 林賽結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和1個女兒的父母。

nameMeanings.name: DE
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: WOODTHORPE
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: English: locative name mainly from Woodthorpe in Strubby (Lincs), though occasionally from Woodthorpe in Loughborough (Leics). There is no evidence to suggest that the surname could be from other like-named places, such as Woodthorpe, Woodthorpe in Staveley (Derbys), Woodthorpe Hall in Handsworth, or Woodthorpe in Sandal Magna (WR Yorks), except perhaps for the early bearer from 1251–2.

nameMeanings.name: ALAN
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Of Celtic origin and uncertain derivation (possibly a diminutive of a word meaning ‘rock’). It was introduced into England by Breton followers of William the Conqueror , most notably Alan , Earl of Brittany, who was rewarded for his services with vast estates in the newly conquered kingdom. In Britain the variants Allan and Allen are considerably less frequent, and generally represent transferred uses of surname forms, whereas in America all three forms of the name are approximately equally common. See also Alun .

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

en.lifeSummary: When ALAN DE WOODTHORPE was born in 1180, in Alford, Lincolnshire, England, his father, William de Woodthorpe I, was 39 and his mother, Cecilia de Craon, was 46. He married Beatrice Farforth in 1180, in Kirton in Lindsey, Lincolnshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 1 daughter.
